commands.review_summary:
Adds an opt-in `skillsMerge: { add, remove }` config for agent entries and Telegram topics, updates schemas/types/generated Telegram metadata, and adds focused unit tests.

PR surface: Source +128, Tests +138. Total +266 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking source-level review finding: on the PR head, `skillsMerge` with no inherited allowlist returns `undefined`, and current workspace loading treats `undefined` as unrestricted. Real behavior proof is still absent for the user-facing feature.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because this PR adds public config/API surface, still needs contributor real behavior proof, and the remaining blockers include product direction as well as code repair.

target.title: feat: add skill allowlist merge config
target.body:
Related #97567

## What Problem This Solves

Resolves a problem where users who configure `agents.defaults.skills` or Telegram group/topic `skills` filters must restate the entire inherited skill allowlist whenever they only want to add or remove a small number of scoped skills.

## Why This Change Was Made

Adds an opt-in `skillsMerge: { add, remove }` config shape for agent entries and Telegram group/direct/topic configs while preserving existing `skills` replacement semantics. This PR treats `skillsMerge` as a concrete proposed contract for the maintainer/product decision called out on #97567: it composes only against a finite inherited filter, because the current runtime filter is allowlist-only and cannot represent "all skills minus X" safely.

## User Impact

Operators can keep a shared baseline in `agents.defaults.skills`, then let an agent add project-specific skills or remove noisy baseline skills without copying the whole list. Telegram forum topics can similarly adjust inherited group/direct skill filters without replacing the complete parent list.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-scope.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts` (3 shards passed: 57 config tests, 46 agent tests, 113 Telegram tests)
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/config/types.skills.ts src/config/types.agents.ts src/config/types.telegram.ts src/config/zod-schema.skills.ts src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/skills/discovery/filter.ts src/skills/discovery/agent-filter.ts src/agents/agent-scope.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/group-config-helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:extensions` (passed; local shell emitted the existing Node engine warning for v22.18.0 vs repo >=22.19.0)
target.author: aditya-vithaldas
